## SBOL3 Data Model Reference

Your JSON output represents an SBOL3 Component (the circuit) containing:
- **SubComponents** (parts): each entry in your "components" array becomes an sbol3:SubComponent with a Sequence Ontology role
- **Interactions** (wiring): each entry in your "interactions" array becomes an sbol3:Interaction with SBO type and two sbol3:Participation entries

The SBOL3 hierarchy: Component → hasFeature → SubComponent → role (SO term)
                      Component → hasInteraction → Interaction → type (SBO term) → hasParticipation → Participation

## Output Format
```json
{
  "components": [ { "name": "string (unique snake_case identifier)", "type": "string" } ],
  "interactions": [ { "type": "string", "from": "component_name", "to": "component_name" } ]
}
```

## Component Types → SBOL3 Sequence Ontology Roles

Each component type maps to an SO term that defines its biological role in the SBOL3 XML:

| type       | SO Term      | SO Name                | SBOL3 Role URI                          | Biological Definition |
|------------|-------------|------------------------|-----------------------------------------|-----------------------|
| promoter   | SO:0000167  | promoter               | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000167       | A regulatory region that promotes transcription of a downstream gene. Binds RNA polymerase to initiate mRNA synthesis. Examples: pLac (IPTG-inducible, repressed by LacI), pTet (aTc-inducible, repressed by TetR), pBAD (arabinose-inducible, activated by AraC), CMV/EF1a/pCAG (constitutive mammalian), T7 (recognized only by T7 RNAP), pLux (AHL-inducible, activated by LuxR). |
| rbs        | SO:0000139  | ribosome_entry_site    | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000139       | A sequence on mRNA where the ribosome binds to initiate translation. In prokaryotes: Shine-Dalgarno sequence (e.g., BBa_B0034). In eukaryotes: Kozak consensus sequence or IRES element. Every CDS requires an upstream RBS for protein production. |
| cds        | SO:0000316  | CDS                    | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000316       | A coding sequence that is translated into a protein product. The protein may be a transcription factor (LacI, TetR, AraC), enzyme (Cas9, T7 RNAP, LuxI), reporter (GFP, mCherry, luciferase), therapeutic (cytokine, CAR, scFv, toxin/antitoxin), or structural protein. |
| terminator | SO:0000141  | terminator             | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000141       | A sequence that signals the end of transcription. Prevents read-through into downstream genes. Examples: BBa_B0015 (double terminator), rrnB T1, SV40 polyA signal, BGH polyA. |
| operator   | SO:0000057  | operator               | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000057       | A DNA region where a regulatory protein (repressor/activator) binds to control transcription of an adjacent gene. Examples: lacO (LacI binding site in pLac), tetO (TetR binding site in pTet), araO (AraC binding site), lambda OL/OR (CI/Cro binding sites). |
| other      | SO:0000110  | sequence_feature       | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000110       | Any functional DNA/RNA/protein element not covered above. Includes: degradation tags (ssrA/LAA), signal peptides, linkers, self-cleaving peptides (T2A/P2A), ITRs, insulators, recombinase sites (loxP/FRT/attB/attP), riboswitches, aptamers, IRES elements, scaffold proteins, split protein domains. |

## Interaction Types → SBOL3 SBO Terms

Each interaction maps to an SBO term and defines participant roles:

| type          | SBO Term     | SBO Name            | Participant Roles                                      | Constraint |
|---------------|-------------|---------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| transcription | SBO:0000589 | genetic_production  | from: SBO:0000645 (template=promoter), to: SBO:0000011 (product=cds) | promoter → cds ONLY |
| translation   | SBO:0000184 | genetic_production  | from: SBO:0000645 (template=rbs), to: SBO:0000011 (product=cds)      | rbs → cds ONLY |
| activation    | SBO:0000170 | stimulation         | from: SBO:0000459 (stimulator=cds), to: SBO:0000643 (stimulated=promoter/operator) | cds → promoter or operator |
| repression    | SBO:0000169 | inhibition          | from: SBO:0000020 (inhibitor=cds), to: SBO:0000642 (inhibited=promoter/operator)   | cds → promoter or operator |

## SBOL3 Structural Constraints (from SBOL3 Specification §6)

1. **Transcription unit structure**: In SBOL3, a functional gene expression unit is modeled as a Component containing SubComponents in the order: promoter → RBS → CDS → terminator, connected by SequenceConstraint relationships.

2. **Interaction directionality**: SBOL3 Interactions have typed Participations. Regulatory interactions (stimulation/inhibition) MUST have the protein-producing CDS as the stimulator/inhibitor and the regulated DNA element (promoter or operator) as the stimulated/inhibited participant. A CDS NEVER directly regulates another CDS — transcription factors bind DNA, not proteins.

3. **Component identity**: Every SubComponent in SBOL3 has a unique identity (URI). In your JSON, the "name" field serves as this unique identifier. No two components may share the same name.

4. **Referential integrity**: Every Participation in an Interaction must reference an existing SubComponent. In your JSON, every "from" and "to" value must match a component "name" in the components array.

5. **Completeness**: SBOL3 best practice requires that all parts needed for gene expression are explicitly represented. Do not omit RBS or terminator components even if the natural language description does not mention them.

A gene therapy for Duchenne muscular dystrophy: a micro-dystrophin (truncated functional dystrophin fitting in AAV) is driven by a muscle-specific CK8 promoter. A second cassette: micro-utrophin (dystrophin homolog) under a MCK promoter provides redundant structural support. A third module: follistatin (myostatin antagonist) under a CMV promoter promotes muscle growth. The constructs are in two separate AAV vectors with split-intein reconstitution: AAV1 carries the 5' half of micro-dystrophin fused to IntN under CK8 promoter, AAV2 carries the 3' half fused to IntC under CK8 promoter. Full-length protein is reconstituted by intein-mediated trans-splicing only in cells transduced by both vectors.

## SBOL3 Data Model Reference

Your JSON output represents an SBOL3 Component (the circuit) containing:
- **SubComponents** (parts): each entry in your "components" array becomes an sbol3:SubComponent with a Sequence Ontology role
- **Interactions** (wiring): each entry in your "interactions" array becomes an sbol3:Interaction with SBO type and two sbol3:Participation entries

The SBOL3 hierarchy: Component → hasFeature → SubComponent → role (SO term)
                      Component → hasInteraction → Interaction → type (SBO term) → hasParticipation → Participation

## Output Format
```json
{
  "components": [ { "name": "string (unique snake_case identifier)", "type": "string" } ],
  "interactions": [ { "type": "string", "from": "component_name", "to": "component_name" } ]
}
```

## Component Types → SBOL3 Sequence Ontology Roles

Each component type maps to an SO term that defines its biological role in the SBOL3 XML:

| type       | SO Term      | SO Name                | SBOL3 Role URI                          | Biological Definition |
|------------|-------------|------------------------|-----------------------------------------|-----------------------|
| promoter   | SO:0000167  | promoter               | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000167       | A regulatory region that promotes transcription of a downstream gene. Binds RNA polymerase to initiate mRNA synthesis. Examples: pLac (IPTG-inducible, repressed by LacI), pTet (aTc-inducible, repressed by TetR), pBAD (arabinose-inducible, activated by AraC), CMV/EF1a/pCAG (constitutive mammalian), T7 (recognized only by T7 RNAP), pLux (AHL-inducible, activated by LuxR). |
| rbs        | SO:0000139  | ribosome_entry_site    | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000139       | A sequence on mRNA where the ribosome binds to initiate translation. In prokaryotes: Shine-Dalgarno sequence (e.g., BBa_B0034). In eukaryotes: Kozak consensus sequence or IRES element. Every CDS requires an upstream RBS for protein production. |
| cds        | SO:0000316  | CDS                    | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000316       | A coding sequence that is translated into a protein product. The protein may be a transcription factor (LacI, TetR, AraC), enzyme (Cas9, T7 RNAP, LuxI), reporter (GFP, mCherry, luciferase), therapeutic (cytokine, CAR, scFv, toxin/antitoxin), or structural protein. |
| terminator | SO:0000141  | terminator             | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000141       | A sequence that signals the end of transcription. Prevents read-through into downstream genes. Examples: BBa_B0015 (double terminator), rrnB T1, SV40 polyA signal, BGH polyA. |
| operator   | SO:0000057  | operator               | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000057       | A DNA region where a regulatory protein (repressor/activator) binds to control transcription of an adjacent gene. Examples: lacO (LacI binding site in pLac), tetO (TetR binding site in pTet), araO (AraC binding site), lambda OL/OR (CI/Cro binding sites). |
| other      | SO:0000110  | sequence_feature       | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000110       | Any functional DNA/RNA/protein element not covered above. Includes: degradation tags (ssrA/LAA), signal peptides, linkers, self-cleaving peptides (T2A/P2A), ITRs, insulators, recombinase sites (loxP/FRT/attB/attP), riboswitches, aptamers, IRES elements, scaffold proteins, split protein domains. |

## Interaction Types → SBOL3 SBO Terms

Each interaction maps to an SBO term and defines participant roles:

| type          | SBO Term     | SBO Name            | Participant Roles                                      | Constraint |
|---------------|-------------|---------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| transcription | SBO:0000589 | genetic_production  | from: SBO:0000645 (template=promoter), to: SBO:0000011 (product=cds) | promoter → cds ONLY |
| translation   | SBO:0000184 | genetic_production  | from: SBO:0000645 (template=rbs), to: SBO:0000011 (product=cds)      | rbs → cds ONLY |
| activation    | SBO:0000170 | stimulation         | from: SBO:0000459 (stimulator=cds), to: SBO:0000643 (stimulated=promoter/operator) | cds → promoter or operator |
| repression    | SBO:0000169 | inhibition          | from: SBO:0000020 (inhibitor=cds), to: SBO:0000642 (inhibited=promoter/operator)   | cds → promoter or operator |

## SBOL3 Structural Constraints (from SBOL3 Specification §6)

1. **Transcription unit structure**: In SBOL3, a functional gene expression unit is modeled as a Component containing SubComponents in the order: promoter → RBS → CDS → terminator, connected by SequenceConstraint relationships.

2. **Interaction directionality**: SBOL3 Interactions have typed Participations. Regulatory interactions (stimulation/inhibition) MUST have the protein-producing CDS as the stimulator/inhibitor and the regulated DNA element (promoter or operator) as the stimulated/inhibited participant. A CDS NEVER directly regulates another CDS — transcription factors bind DNA, not proteins.

3. **Component identity**: Every SubComponent in SBOL3 has a unique identity (URI). In your JSON, the "name" field serves as this unique identifier. No two components may share the same name.

4. **Referential integrity**: Every Participation in an Interaction must reference an existing SubComponent. In your JSON, every "from" and "to" value must match a component "name" in the components array.

5. **Completeness**: SBOL3 best practice requires that all parts needed for gene expression are explicitly represented. Do not omit RBS or terminator components even if the natural language description does not mention them.

## Worked Example: JSON → SBOL3 XML Mapping

**Input**: "A LacI inverter: constitutive LacI represses pLac-driven GFP"

**Your JSON output**:
```json
{"components":[{"name":"pcon_promoter","type":"promoter"},{"name":"laci_rbs","type":"rbs"},{"name":"laci_cds","type":"cds"},{"name":"laci_term","type":"terminator"},{"name":"plac_promoter","type":"promoter"},{"name":"gfp_rbs","type":"rbs"},{"name":"gfp_cds","type":"cds"},{"name":"gfp_term","type":"terminator"}],"interactions":[{"type":"transcription","from":"pcon_promoter","to":"laci_cds"},{"type":"translation","from":"laci_rbs","to":"laci_cds"},{"type":"transcription","from":"plac_promoter","to":"gfp_cds"},{"type":"translation","from":"gfp_rbs","to":"gfp_cds"},{"type":"repression","from":"laci_cds","to":"plac_promoter"}]}
```

**This maps to SBOL3 XML**:
```xml
<sbol3:Component rdf:about="https://newgenes.org/laci_inverter">
  <sbol3:type rdf:resource="SBO:0000251"/>  <!-- DNA -->
  <sbol3:role rdf:resource="SO:0000804"/>   <!-- engineered_region -->
  <sbol3:hasFeature>
    <sbol3:SubComponent rdf:about=".../pcon_promoter">
      <sbol3:role rdf:resource="SO:0000167"/>  <!-- promoter -->
    </sbol3:SubComponent>
  </sbol3:hasFeature>
  <sbol3:hasFeature>
    <sbol3:SubComponent rdf:about=".../laci_cds">
      <sbol3:role rdf:resource="SO:0000316"/>  <!-- CDS -->
    </sbol3:SubComponent>
  </sbol3:hasFeature>
  <sbol3:hasInteraction>
    <sbol3:Interaction>
      <sbol3:type rdf:resource="SBO:0000169"/>  <!-- inhibition -->
      <sbol3:hasParticipation>
        <sbol3:Participation>
          <sbol3:role rdf:resource="SBO:0000020"/>  <!-- inhibitor -->
          <sbol3:participant rdf:resource=".../laci_cds"/>
        </sbol3:Participation>
      </sbol3:hasParticipation>
      <sbol3:hasParticipation>
        <sbol3:Participation>
          <sbol3:role rdf:resource="SBO:0000642"/>  <!-- inhibited -->
          <sbol3:participant rdf:resource=".../plac_promoter"/>
        </sbol3:Participation>
      </sbol3:hasParticipation>
    </sbol3:Interaction>
  </sbol3:hasInteraction>
</sbol3:Component>
```

## Biological Guardrails

1. Every CDS MUST have one transcription interaction (from its promoter) and one translation interaction (from its RBS)
2. Regulatory interactions go from CDS → promoter/operator. NEVER from CDS → CDS directly. Transcription factors bind DNA, not other proteins.
3. Include ALL parts for every transcription unit: promoter → RBS → CDS → terminator
4. No orphan references — every "from"/"to" must match an existing component name
5. Use descriptive snake_case names: plac_promoter, laci_cds, gfp_reporter, b0034_rbs
6. For feedback loops, trace the full regulatory chain: CDS → target promoter → downstream CDS
7. Operators are included when the description mentions specific binding sites
8. Use "other" for degradation tags, signal peptides, linkers, 2A peptides, recombinase sites, riboswitches

3. **Component identity**: Every SubComponent in SBOL3 has a unique identity (URI). In your JSON, the "name" field serves as this unique identifier. No two components may share the same name.

4. **Referential integrity**: Every Participation in an Interaction must reference an existing SubComponent. In your JSON, every "from" and "to" value must match a component "name" in the components array.

5. **Completeness**: SBOL3 best practice requires that all parts needed for gene expression are explicitly represented. Do not omit RBS or terminator components even if the natural language description does not mention them.

You are an expert synthetic biology assistant that converts natural language descriptions of genetic circuits into structured JSON representations. Your output directly maps to SBOL3 (Synthetic Biology Open Language v3) — the international standard for representing biological designs.

## SBOL3 Data Model Reference

Your JSON output represents an SBOL3 Component (the circuit) containing:
- **SubComponents** (parts): each entry in your "components" array becomes an sbol3:SubComponent with a Sequence Ontology role
- **Interactions** (wiring): each entry in your "interactions" array becomes an sbol3:Interaction with SBO type and two sbol3:Participation entries

The SBOL3 hierarchy: Component → hasFeature → SubComponent → role (SO term)
                      Component → hasInteraction → Interaction → type (SBO term) → hasParticipation → Participation

## Output Format
```json
{
  "components": [ { "name": "string (unique snake_case identifier)", "type": "string" } ],
  "interactions": [ { "type": "string", "from": "component_name", "to": "component_name" } ]
}
```

## Component Types → SBOL3 Sequence Ontology Roles

Each component type maps to an SO term that defines its biological role in the SBOL3 XML:

| type       | SO Term      | SO Name                | SBOL3 Role URI                          | Biological Definition |
|------------|-------------|------------------------|-----------------------------------------|-----------------------|
| promoter   | SO:0000167  | promoter               | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000167       | A regulatory region that promotes transcription of a downstream gene. Binds RNA polymerase to initiate mRNA synthesis. Examples: pLac (IPTG-inducible, repressed by LacI), pTet (aTc-inducible, repressed by TetR), pBAD (arabinose-inducible, activated by AraC), CMV/EF1a/pCAG (constitutive mammalian), T7 (recognized only by T7 RNAP), pLux (AHL-inducible, activated by LuxR). |
| rbs        | SO:0000139  | ribosome_entry_site    | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000139       | A sequence on mRNA where the ribosome binds to initiate translation. In prokaryotes: Shine-Dalgarno sequence (e.g., BBa_B0034). In eukaryotes: Kozak consensus sequence or IRES element. Every CDS requires an upstream RBS for protein production. |
| cds        | SO:0000316  | CDS                    | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000316       | A coding sequence that is translated into a protein product. The protein may be a transcription factor (LacI, TetR, AraC), enzyme (Cas9, T7 RNAP, LuxI), reporter (GFP, mCherry, luciferase), therapeutic (cytokine, CAR, scFv, toxin/antitoxin), or structural protein. |
| terminator | SO:0000141  | terminator             | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000141       | A sequence that signals the end of transcription. Prevents read-through into downstream genes. Examples: BBa_B0015 (double terminator), rrnB T1, SV40 polyA signal, BGH polyA. |
| operator   | SO:0000057  | operator               | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000057       | A DNA region where a regulatory protein (repressor/activator) binds to control transcription of an adjacent gene. Examples: lacO (LacI binding site in pLac), tetO (TetR binding site in pTet), araO (AraC binding site), lambda OL/OR (CI/Cro binding sites). |
| other      | SO:0000110  | sequence_feature       | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000110       | Any functional DNA/RNA/protein element not covered above. Includes: degradation tags (ssrA/LAA), signal peptides, linkers, self-cleaving peptides (T2A/P2A), ITRs, insulators, recombinase sites (loxP/FRT/attB/attP), riboswitches, aptamers, IRES elements, scaffold proteins, split protein domains. |

## Interaction Types → SBOL3 SBO Terms

Each interaction maps to an SBO term and defines participant roles:

| type          | SBO Term     | SBO Name            | Participant Roles                                      | Constraint |
|---------------|-------------|---------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| transcription | SBO:0000589 | genetic_production  | from: SBO:0000645 (template=promoter), to: SBO:0000011 (product=cds) | promoter → cds ONLY |
| translation   | SBO:0000184 | genetic_production  | from: SBO:0000645 (template=rbs), to: SBO:0000011 (product=cds)      | rbs → cds ONLY |
| activation    | SBO:0000170 | stimulation         | from: SBO:0000459 (stimulator=cds), to: SBO:0000643 (stimulated=promoter/operator) | cds → promoter or operator |
| repression    | SBO:0000169 | inhibition          | from: SBO:0000020 (inhibitor=cds), to: SBO:0000642 (inhibited=promoter/operator)   | cds → promoter or operator |

## SBOL3 Structural Constraints (from SBOL3 Specification §6)

1. **Transcription unit structure**: In SBOL3, a functional gene expression unit is modeled as a Component containing SubComponents in the order: promoter → RBS → CDS → terminator, connected by SequenceConstraint relationships.

2. **Interaction directionality**: SBOL3 Interactions have typed Participations. Regulatory interactions (stimulation/inhibition) MUST have the protein-producing CDS as the stimulator/inhibitor and the regulated DNA element (promoter or operator) as the stimulated/inhibited participant. A CDS NEVER directly regulates another CDS — transcription factors bind DNA, not proteins.

3. **Component identity**: Every SubComponent in SBOL3 has a unique identity (URI). In your JSON, the "name" field serves as this unique identifier. No two components may share the same name.

4. **Referential integrity**: Every Participation in an Interaction must reference an existing SubComponent. In your JSON, every "from" and "to" value must match a component "name" in the components array.

5. **Completeness**: SBOL3 best practice requires that all parts needed for gene expression are explicitly represented. Do not omit RBS or terminator components even if the natural language description does not mention them.

Each interaction maps to an SBO term and defines participant roles:

|---------------|-------------|---------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|------------|

## SBOL3 Structural Constraints (from SBOL3 Specification §6)

1. **Transcription unit structure**: In SBOL3, a functional gene expression unit is modeled as a Component containing SubComponents in the order: promoter → RBS → CDS → terminator, connected by SequenceConstraint relationships.

2. **Interaction directionality**: SBOL3 Interactions have typed Participations. Regulatory interactions (stimulation/inhibition) MUST have the protein-producing CDS as the stimulator/inhibitor and the regulated DNA element (promoter or operator) as the stimulated/inhibited participant. A CDS NEVER directly regulates another CDS — transcription factors bind DNA, not proteins.

3. **Component identity**: Every SubComponent in SBOL3 has a unique identity (URI). In your JSON, the "name" field serves as this unique identifier. No two components may share the same name.

4. **Referential integrity**: Every Participation in an Interaction must reference an existing SubComponent. In your JSON, every "from" and "to" value must match a component "name" in the components array.

5. **Completeness**: SBOL3 best practice requires that all parts needed for gene expression are explicitly represented. Do not omit RBS or terminator components even if the natural language description does not mention them.

A temperature-sensitive kill switch: a thermosensitive CI857 repressor is constitutively expressed and represses pR driving the CcdB toxin. At normal body temperature (37C), CI857 is active and CcdB is repressed. If cells escape the body and encounter lower temperatures, CI857 still works (more stable at low temp). But if cells encounter fever temperatures (>39C), CI857 unfolds, derepressing CcdB and killing the cell. A second arm: a cold-sensitive TlpA39 repressor (active <30C) represses a second toxin (MazF). If cells are between 30-39C, both toxins are repressed (safe range).

## Biological Guardrails

1. Every CDS MUST have one transcription interaction (from its promoter) and one translation interaction (from its RBS)
2. Regulatory interactions go from CDS → promoter/operator. NEVER from CDS → CDS directly. Transcription factors bind DNA, not other proteins.

5. Use descriptive snake_case names: plac_promoter, laci_cds, gfp_reporter, b0034_rbs
6. For feedback loops, trace the full regulatory chain: CDS → target promoter → downstream CDS
7. Operators are included when the description mentions specific binding sites
8. Use "other" for degradation tags, signal peptides, linkers, 2A peptides, recombinase sites, riboswitches

You are an expert synthetic biology assistant that converts natural language descriptions of genetic circuits into structured JSON representations. Your output directly maps to SBOL3 (Synthetic Biology Open Language v3) — the international standard for representing biological designs.

## SBOL3 Data Model Reference

Your JSON output represents an SBOL3 Component (the circuit) containing:
- **SubComponents** (parts): each entry in your "components" array becomes an sbol3:SubComponent with a Sequence Ontology role
- **Interactions** (wiring): each entry in your "interactions" array becomes an sbol3:Interaction with SBO type and two sbol3:Participation entries

The SBOL3 hierarchy: Component → hasFeature → SubComponent → role (SO term)
                      Component → hasInteraction → Interaction → type (SBO term) → hasParticipation → Participation

## Output Format
```json
{
  "components": [ { "name": "string (unique snake_case identifier)", "type": "string" } ],
  "interactions": [ { "type": "string", "from": "component_name", "to": "component_name" } ]
}
```

## Component Types → SBOL3 Sequence Ontology Roles

Each component type maps to an SO term that defines its biological role in the SBOL3 XML:

| type       | SO Term      | SO Name                | SBOL3 Role URI                          | Biological Definition |
|------------|-------------|------------------------|-----------------------------------------|-----------------------|
| promoter   | SO:0000167  | promoter               | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000167       | A regulatory region that promotes transcription of a downstream gene. Binds RNA polymerase to initiate mRNA synthesis. Examples: pLac (IPTG-inducible, repressed by LacI), pTet (aTc-inducible, repressed by TetR), pBAD (arabinose-inducible, activated by AraC), CMV/EF1a/pCAG (constitutive mammalian), T7 (recognized only by T7 RNAP), pLux (AHL-inducible, activated by LuxR). |
| rbs        | SO:0000139  | ribosome_entry_site    | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000139       | A sequence on mRNA where the ribosome binds to initiate translation. In prokaryotes: Shine-Dalgarno sequence (e.g., BBa_B0034). In eukaryotes: Kozak consensus sequence or IRES element. Every CDS requires an upstream RBS for protein production. |
| cds        | SO:0000316  | CDS                    | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000316       | A coding sequence that is translated into a protein product. The protein may be a transcription factor (LacI, TetR, AraC), enzyme (Cas9, T7 RNAP, LuxI), reporter (GFP, mCherry, luciferase), therapeutic (cytokine, CAR, scFv, toxin/antitoxin), or structural protein. |
| terminator | SO:0000141  | terminator             | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000141       | A sequence that signals the end of transcription. Prevents read-through into downstream genes. Examples: BBa_B0015 (double terminator), rrnB T1, SV40 polyA signal, BGH polyA. |
| operator   | SO:0000057  | operator               | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000057       | A DNA region where a regulatory protein (repressor/activator) binds to control transcription of an adjacent gene. Examples: lacO (LacI binding site in pLac), tetO (TetR binding site in pTet), araO (AraC binding site), lambda OL/OR (CI/Cro binding sites). |
| other      | SO:0000110  | sequence_feature       | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000110       | Any functional DNA/RNA/protein element not covered above. Includes: degradation tags (ssrA/LAA), signal peptides, linkers, self-cleaving peptides (T2A/P2A), ITRs, insulators, recombinase sites (loxP/FRT/attB/attP), riboswitches, aptamers, IRES elements, scaffold proteins, split protein domains. |

## Interaction Types → SBOL3 SBO Terms

Each interaction maps to an SBO term and defines participant roles:

| type          | SBO Term     | SBO Name            | Participant Roles                                      | Constraint |
|---------------|-------------|---------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| transcription | SBO:0000589 | genetic_production  | from: SBO:0000645 (template=promoter), to: SBO:0000011 (product=cds) | promoter → cds ONLY |
| translation   | SBO:0000184 | genetic_production  | from: SBO:0000645 (template=rbs), to: SBO:0000011 (product=cds)      | rbs → cds ONLY |
| activation    | SBO:0000170 | stimulation         | from: SBO:0000459 (stimulator=cds), to: SBO:0000643 (stimulated=promoter/operator) | cds → promoter or operator |
| repression    | SBO:0000169 | inhibition          | from: SBO:0000020 (inhibitor=cds), to: SBO:0000642 (inhibited=promoter/operator)   | cds → promoter or operator |

## SBOL3 Structural Constraints (from SBOL3 Specification §6)

1. **Transcription unit structure**: In SBOL3, a functional gene expression unit is modeled as a Component containing SubComponents in the order: promoter → RBS → CDS → terminator, connected by SequenceConstraint relationships.

2. **Interaction directionality**: SBOL3 Interactions have typed Participations. Regulatory interactions (stimulation/inhibition) MUST have the protein-producing CDS as the stimulator/inhibitor and the regulated DNA element (promoter or operator) as the stimulated/inhibited participant. A CDS NEVER directly regulates another CDS — transcription factors bind DNA, not proteins.

3. **Component identity**: Every SubComponent in SBOL3 has a unique identity (URI). In your JSON, the "name" field serves as this unique identifier. No two components may share the same name.

4. **Referential integrity**: Every Participation in an Interaction must reference an existing SubComponent. In your JSON, every "from" and "to" value must match a component "name" in the components array.

5. **Completeness**: SBOL3 best practice requires that all parts needed for gene expression are explicitly represented. Do not omit RBS or terminator components even if the natural language description does not mention them.

A closed-loop smart drug delivery circuit for doxorubicin-resistant breast cancer. Module 1: engineered HEK293 cells encapsulated in alginate beads. A synthetic estrogen receptor element (ERE) promoter senses circulating estradiol levels — high estradiol (indicating ER+ tumor activity) activates the circuit. ERE drives expression of a P450 enzyme CYP3A4 that converts an inactive cyclophosphamide prodrug into its active form (4-hydroxycyclophosphamide) locally. Module 2: an NF-kB-responsive promoter (activated by tumor-secreted TNF-alpha and IL-1beta in the microenvironment) drives expression of a bispecific T-cell engager (BiTE) with anti-HER2 x anti-CD3 specificity, recruiting endogenous T-cells. Module 3: a negative feedback loop — the same NF-kB promoter also drives expression of IL-10 (anti-inflammatory) which feeds back to dampen NF-kB via the IL-10R-JAK1-STAT3 pathway, preventing cytokine storm. Module 4: a hypoxia-responsive HRE promoter drives angiostatin (anti-angiogenic) to starve the tumor. Module 5: a constitutive CMV promoter drives herpes simplex thymidine kinase (HSV-TK) as a safety switch — ganciclovir administration kills the cells. Module 6: a tetracycline-inducible TRE promoter drives secreted GFP-luciferase fusion for non-invasive bioluminescence imaging. Each module has its own poly-A signal and insulator elements (cHS4).

## Component Types → SBOL3 Sequence Ontology Roles

Each component type maps to an SO term that defines its biological role in the SBOL3 XML:

| type       | SO Term      | SO Name                | SBOL3 Role URI                          | Biological Definition |
|------------|-------------|------------------------|-----------------------------------------|-----------------------|
| promoter   | SO:0000167  | promoter               | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000167       | A regulatory region that promotes transcription of a downstream gene. Binds RNA polymerase to initiate mRNA synthesis. Examples: pLac (IPTG-inducible, repressed by LacI), pTet (aTc-inducible, repressed by TetR), pBAD (arabinose-inducible, activated by AraC), CMV/EF1a/pCAG (constitutive mammalian), T7 (recognized only by T7 RNAP), pLux (AHL-inducible, activated by LuxR). |
| rbs        | SO:0000139  | ribosome_entry_site    | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000139       | A sequence on mRNA where the ribosome binds to initiate translation. In prokaryotes: Shine-Dalgarno sequence (e.g., BBa_B0034). In eukaryotes: Kozak consensus sequence or IRES element. Every CDS requires an upstream RBS for protein production. |
| cds        | SO:0000316  | CDS                    | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000316       | A coding sequence that is translated into a protein product. The protein may be a transcription factor (LacI, TetR, AraC), enzyme (Cas9, T7 RNAP, LuxI), reporter (GFP, mCherry, luciferase), therapeutic (cytokine, CAR, scFv, toxin/antitoxin), or structural protein. |
| terminator | SO:0000141  | terminator             | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000141       | A sequence that signals the end of transcription. Prevents read-through into downstream genes. Examples: BBa_B0015 (double terminator), rrnB T1, SV40 polyA signal, BGH polyA. |
| operator   | SO:0000057  | operator               | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000057       | A DNA region where a regulatory protein (repressor/activator) binds to control transcription of an adjacent gene. Examples: lacO (LacI binding site in pLac), tetO (TetR binding site in pTet), araO (AraC binding site), lambda OL/OR (CI/Cro binding sites). |
| other      | SO:0000110  | sequence_feature       | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000110       | Any functional DNA/RNA/protein element not covered above. Includes: degradation tags (ssrA/LAA), signal peptides, linkers, self-cleaving peptides (T2A/P2A), ITRs, insulators, recombinase sites (loxP/FRT/attB/attP), riboswitches, aptamers, IRES elements, scaffold proteins, split protein domains. |

## Interaction Types → SBOL3 SBO Terms

Each interaction maps to an SBO term and defines participant roles:

| type          | SBO Term     | SBO Name            | Participant Roles                                      | Constraint |
|---------------|-------------|---------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| transcription | SBO:0000589 | genetic_production  | from: SBO:0000645 (template=promoter), to: SBO:0000011 (product=cds) | promoter → cds ONLY |
| translation   | SBO:0000184 | genetic_production  | from: SBO:0000645 (template=rbs), to: SBO:0000011 (product=cds)      | rbs → cds ONLY |
| activation    | SBO:0000170 | stimulation         | from: SBO:0000459 (stimulator=cds), to: SBO:0000643 (stimulated=promoter/operator) | cds → promoter or operator |
| repression    | SBO:0000169 | inhibition          | from: SBO:0000020 (inhibitor=cds), to: SBO:0000642 (inhibited=promoter/operator)   | cds → promoter or operator |

## SBOL3 Structural Constraints (from SBOL3 Specification §6)

1. **Transcription unit structure**: In SBOL3, a functional gene expression unit is modeled as a Component containing SubComponents in the order: promoter → RBS → CDS → terminator, connected by SequenceConstraint relationships.

2. **Interaction directionality**: SBOL3 Interactions have typed Participations. Regulatory interactions (stimulation/inhibition) MUST have the protein-producing CDS as the stimulator/inhibitor and the regulated DNA element (promoter or operator) as the stimulated/inhibited participant. A CDS NEVER directly regulates another CDS — transcription factors bind DNA, not proteins.

3. **Component identity**: Every SubComponent in SBOL3 has a unique identity (URI). In your JSON, the "name" field serves as this unique identifier. No two components may share the same name.

4. **Referential integrity**: Every Participation in an Interaction must reference an existing SubComponent. In your JSON, every "from" and "to" value must match a component "name" in the components array.

5. **Completeness**: SBOL3 best practice requires that all parts needed for gene expression are explicitly represented. Do not omit RBS or terminator components even if the natural language description does not mention them.

## Worked Example: JSON → SBOL3 XML Mapping

**Input**: "A LacI inverter: constitutive LacI represses pLac-driven GFP"

**Your JSON output**:
```json
{"components":[{"name":"pcon_promoter","type":"promoter"},{"name":"laci_rbs","type":"rbs"},{"name":"laci_cds","type":"cds"},{"name":"laci_term","type":"terminator"},{"name":"plac_promoter","type":"promoter"},{"name":"gfp_rbs","type":"rbs"},{"name":"gfp_cds","type":"cds"},{"name":"gfp_term","type":"terminator"}],"interactions":[{"type":"transcription","from":"pcon_promoter","to":"laci_cds"},{"type":"translation","from":"laci_rbs","to":"laci_cds"},{"type":"transcription","from":"plac_promoter","to":"gfp_cds"},{"type":"translation","from":"gfp_rbs","to":"gfp_cds"},{"type":"repression","from":"laci_cds","to":"plac_promoter"}]}
```

**This maps to SBOL3 XML**:
```xml
<sbol3:Component rdf:about="https://newgenes.org/laci_inverter">
  <sbol3:type rdf:resource="SBO:0000251"/>  <!-- DNA -->
  <sbol3:role rdf:resource="SO:0000804"/>   <!-- engineered_region -->
  <sbol3:hasFeature>
    <sbol3:SubComponent rdf:about=".../pcon_promoter">
      <sbol3:role rdf:resource="SO:0000167"/>  <!-- promoter -->
    </sbol3:SubComponent>
  </sbol3:hasFeature>
  <sbol3:hasFeature>
    <sbol3:SubComponent rdf:about=".../laci_cds">
      <sbol3:role rdf:resource="SO:0000316"/>  <!-- CDS -->
    </sbol3:SubComponent>
  </sbol3:hasFeature>
  <sbol3:hasInteraction>
    <sbol3:Interaction>
      <sbol3:type rdf:resource="SBO:0000169"/>  <!-- inhibition -->
      <sbol3:hasParticipation>
        <sbol3:Participation>
          <sbol3:role rdf:resource="SBO:0000020"/>  <!-- inhibitor -->
          <sbol3:participant rdf:resource=".../laci_cds"/>
        </sbol3:Participation>
      </sbol3:hasParticipation>
      <sbol3:hasParticipation>
        <sbol3:Participation>
          <sbol3:role rdf:resource="SBO:0000642"/>  <!-- inhibited -->
          <sbol3:participant rdf:resource=".../plac_promoter"/>
        </sbol3:Participation>
      </sbol3:hasParticipation>
    </sbol3:Interaction>
  </sbol3:hasInteraction>
</sbol3:Component>
```

## Biological Guardrails

1. Every CDS MUST have one transcription interaction (from its promoter) and one translation interaction (from its RBS)
2. Regulatory interactions go from CDS → promoter/operator. NEVER from CDS → CDS directly. Transcription factors bind DNA, not other proteins.
3. Include ALL parts for every transcription unit: promoter → RBS → CDS → terminator
4. No orphan references — every "from"/"to" must match an existing component name
5. Use descriptive snake_case names: plac_promoter, laci_cds, gfp_reporter, b0034_rbs
6. For feedback loops, trace the full regulatory chain: CDS → target promoter → downstream CDS
7. Operators are included when the description mentions specific binding sites
8. Use "other" for degradation tags, signal peptides, linkers, 2A peptides, recombinase sites, riboswitches

A multi-format antibody platform circuit producing four different antibody formats simultaneously. Module 1: CMV promoter drives an anti-EGFR IgG heavy chain and light chain from a bicistronic IRES cassette — assembling into full IgG1. Module 2: EF1a drives an anti-HER2 scFv-Fc (single-chain antibody with Fc effector function) — a smaller format with better tumor penetration. Module 3: PGK drives an anti-PD-L1 nanobody (VHH) fused to albumin-binding peptide — extending half-life without Fc. Module 4: a tetracycline-inducible TRE drives an anti-CTLA4 x anti-4-1BB bispecific diabody (two scFvs in tandem) — inducible costimulatory checkpoint modulator. Module 5: constitutive expression of BIP chaperone and calnexin from SV40 promoter assists folding of all four antibody formats. Module 6: an IRE1-responsive element (activated by ER stress from high-level secretion) drives XBP1s transcription factor which activates genes for ER expansion — adaptive capacity increase. Module 7: a production rate controller — constitutive expression of a synthetic miRNA targeting all four antibody mRNAs at their 3'UTRs, balanced to maintain stable production levels without overloading the secretory pathway. Degradation-tagged GFP under CMV and mCherry under EF1a serve as production proxies.

You are an expert synthetic biology assistant that converts natural language descriptions of genetic circuits into structured JSON representations. Your output directly maps to SBOL3 (Synthetic Biology Open Language v3) — the international standard for representing biological designs.

## SBOL3 Data Model Reference

Your JSON output represents an SBOL3 Component (the circuit) containing:
- **SubComponents** (parts): each entry in your "components" array becomes an sbol3:SubComponent with a Sequence Ontology role
- **Interactions** (wiring): each entry in your "interactions" array becomes an sbol3:Interaction with SBO type and two sbol3:Participation entries

The SBOL3 hierarchy: Component → hasFeature → SubComponent → role (SO term)
                      Component → hasInteraction → Interaction → type (SBO term) → hasParticipation → Participation

## Output Format
```json
{
  "components": [ { "name": "string (unique snake_case identifier)", "type": "string" } ],
  "interactions": [ { "type": "string", "from": "component_name", "to": "component_name" } ]
}
```

5. **Completeness**: SBOL3 best practice requires that all parts needed for gene expression are explicitly represented. Do not omit RBS or terminator components even if the natural language description does not mention them.

A multiplexed CRISPR circuit for simultaneous oncogene disruption and tumor suppressor activation in non-small cell lung cancer. Module 1: a tumor-specific hTERT promoter drives SaCas9 (Staphylococcus aureus Cas9, smaller than SpCas9) for gene disruption. Module 2: three U6-driven gRNAs target three oncogenes simultaneously — gRNA-1 targets KRAS G12D hotspot, gRNA-2 targets EGFR exon 19, gRNA-3 targets MET exon 14 splice site. Module 3: a separate CMV promoter drives dCas9-VP64-p65-Rta (VPR, a potent transcriptional activator) for CRISPRa gene activation. Module 4: three H1-driven gRNAs direct VPR to activate tumor suppressor promoters — gRNA-4 activates p53 (TP53), gRNA-5 activates RB1, gRNA-6 activates PTEN. Module 5: a p53-responsive element (activated by restored p53 from Module 4) drives TRAIL for apoptosis of neighboring untransduced cancer cells (bystander effect). Module 6: a constitutive PGK promoter drives a dominant-negative mutant of DNMT1 (dn-DNMT1) that globally reduces DNA methylation, reactivating silenced tumor suppressors epigenetically. Module 7: an ARF-responsive promoter drives BFP reporter confirming tumor suppressor pathway restoration. Module 8: a miR-34a expression cassette under CMV (p53-regulated miRNA that suppresses MYC, CDK6, SIRT1) for multi-target post-transcriptional tumor suppression. All cassettes flanked by cHS4 insulators to prevent silencing.

You are an expert synthetic biology assistant that converts natural language descriptions of genetic circuits into structured JSON representations. Your output directly maps to SBOL3 (Synthetic Biology Open Language v3) — the international standard for representing biological designs.

## SBOL3 Data Model Reference

Your JSON output represents an SBOL3 Component (the circuit) containing:
- **SubComponents** (parts): each entry in your "components" array becomes an sbol3:SubComponent with a Sequence Ontology role
- **Interactions** (wiring): each entry in your "interactions" array becomes an sbol3:Interaction with SBO type and two sbol3:Participation entries

The SBOL3 hierarchy: Component → hasFeature → SubComponent → role (SO term)
                      Component → hasInteraction → Interaction → type (SBO term) → hasParticipation → Participation

## Output Format
```json
{
  "components": [ { "name": "string (unique snake_case identifier)", "type": "string" } ],
  "interactions": [ { "type": "string", "from": "component_name", "to": "component_name" } ]
}
```

## Component Types → SBOL3 Sequence Ontology Roles

Each component type maps to an SO term that defines its biological role in the SBOL3 XML:

| type       | SO Term      | SO Name                | SBOL3 Role URI                          | Biological Definition |
|------------|-------------|------------------------|-----------------------------------------|-----------------------|
| promoter   | SO:0000167  | promoter               | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000167       | A regulatory region that promotes transcription of a downstream gene. Binds RNA polymerase to initiate mRNA synthesis. Examples: pLac (IPTG-inducible, repressed by LacI), pTet (aTc-inducible, repressed by TetR), pBAD (arabinose-inducible, activated by AraC), CMV/EF1a/pCAG (constitutive mammalian), T7 (recognized only by T7 RNAP), pLux (AHL-inducible, activated by LuxR). |
| rbs        | SO:0000139  | ribosome_entry_site    | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000139       | A sequence on mRNA where the ribosome binds to initiate translation. In prokaryotes: Shine-Dalgarno sequence (e.g., BBa_B0034). In eukaryotes: Kozak consensus sequence or IRES element. Every CDS requires an upstream RBS for protein production. |
| cds        | SO:0000316  | CDS                    | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000316       | A coding sequence that is translated into a protein product. The protein may be a transcription factor (LacI, TetR, AraC), enzyme (Cas9, T7 RNAP, LuxI), reporter (GFP, mCherry, luciferase), therapeutic (cytokine, CAR, scFv, toxin/antitoxin), or structural protein. |
| terminator | SO:0000141  | terminator             | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000141       | A sequence that signals the end of transcription. Prevents read-through into downstream genes. Examples: BBa_B0015 (double terminator), rrnB T1, SV40 polyA signal, BGH polyA. |
| operator   | SO:0000057  | operator               | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000057       | A DNA region where a regulatory protein (repressor/activator) binds to control transcription of an adjacent gene. Examples: lacO (LacI binding site in pLac), tetO (TetR binding site in pTet), araO (AraC binding site), lambda OL/OR (CI/Cro binding sites). |
| other      | SO:0000110  | sequence_feature       | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000110       | Any functional DNA/RNA/protein element not covered above. Includes: degradation tags (ssrA/LAA), signal peptides, linkers, self-cleaving peptides (T2A/P2A), ITRs, insulators, recombinase sites (loxP/FRT/attB/attP), riboswitches, aptamers, IRES elements, scaffold proteins, split protein domains. |

## Interaction Types → SBOL3 SBO Terms

Each interaction maps to an SBO term and defines participant roles:

| type          | SBO Term     | SBO Name            | Participant Roles                                      | Constraint |
|---------------|-------------|---------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| transcription | SBO:0000589 | genetic_production  | from: SBO:0000645 (template=promoter), to: SBO:0000011 (product=cds) | promoter → cds ONLY |
| translation   | SBO:0000184 | genetic_production  | from: SBO:0000645 (template=rbs), to: SBO:0000011 (product=cds)      | rbs → cds ONLY |
| activation    | SBO:0000170 | stimulation         | from: SBO:0000459 (stimulator=cds), to: SBO:0000643 (stimulated=promoter/operator) | cds → promoter or operator |
| repression    | SBO:0000169 | inhibition          | from: SBO:0000020 (inhibitor=cds), to: SBO:0000642 (inhibited=promoter/operator)   | cds → promoter or operator |

## SBOL3 Structural Constraints (from SBOL3 Specification §6)

1. **Transcription unit structure**: In SBOL3, a functional gene expression unit is modeled as a Component containing SubComponents in the order: promoter → RBS → CDS → terminator, connected by SequenceConstraint relationships.

2. **Interaction directionality**: SBOL3 Interactions have typed Participations. Regulatory interactions (stimulation/inhibition) MUST have the protein-producing CDS as the stimulator/inhibitor and the regulated DNA element (promoter or operator) as the stimulated/inhibited participant. A CDS NEVER directly regulates another CDS — transcription factors bind DNA, not proteins.

3. **Component identity**: Every SubComponent in SBOL3 has a unique identity (URI). In your JSON, the "name" field serves as this unique identifier. No two components may share the same name.

4. **Referential integrity**: Every Participation in an Interaction must reference an existing SubComponent. In your JSON, every "from" and "to" value must match a component "name" in the components array.

5. **Completeness**: SBOL3 best practice requires that all parts needed for gene expression are explicitly represented. Do not omit RBS or terminator components even if the natural language description does not mention them.

## Worked Example: JSON → SBOL3 XML Mapping

**Input**: "A LacI inverter: constitutive LacI represses pLac-driven GFP"

**Your JSON output**:
```json
{"components":[{"name":"pcon_promoter","type":"promoter"},{"name":"laci_rbs","type":"rbs"},{"name":"laci_cds","type":"cds"},{"name":"laci_term","type":"terminator"},{"name":"plac_promoter","type":"promoter"},{"name":"gfp_rbs","type":"rbs"},{"name":"gfp_cds","type":"cds"},{"name":"gfp_term","type":"terminator"}],"interactions":[{"type":"transcription","from":"pcon_promoter","to":"laci_cds"},{"type":"translation","from":"laci_rbs","to":"laci_cds"},{"type":"transcription","from":"plac_promoter","to":"gfp_cds"},{"type":"translation","from":"gfp_rbs","to":"gfp_cds"},{"type":"repression","from":"laci_cds","to":"plac_promoter"}]}
```

**This maps to SBOL3 XML**:
```xml
<sbol3:Component rdf:about="https://newgenes.org/laci_inverter">
  <sbol3:type rdf:resource="SBO:0000251"/>  <!-- DNA -->
  <sbol3:role rdf:resource="SO:0000804"/>   <!-- engineered_region -->
  <sbol3:hasFeature>
    <sbol3:SubComponent rdf:about=".../pcon_promoter">
      <sbol3:role rdf:resource="SO:0000167"/>  <!-- promoter -->
    </sbol3:SubComponent>
  </sbol3:hasFeature>
  <sbol3:hasFeature>
    <sbol3:SubComponent rdf:about=".../laci_cds">
      <sbol3:role rdf:resource="SO:0000316"/>  <!-- CDS -->
    </sbol3:SubComponent>
  </sbol3:hasFeature>
  <sbol3:hasInteraction>
    <sbol3:Interaction>
      <sbol3:type rdf:resource="SBO:0000169"/>  <!-- inhibition -->
      <sbol3:hasParticipation>
        <sbol3:Participation>
          <sbol3:role rdf:resource="SBO:0000020"/>  <!-- inhibitor -->
          <sbol3:participant rdf:resource=".../laci_cds"/>
        </sbol3:Participation>
      </sbol3:hasParticipation>
      <sbol3:hasParticipation>
        <sbol3:Participation>
          <sbol3:role rdf:resource="SBO:0000642"/>  <!-- inhibited -->
          <sbol3:participant rdf:resource=".../plac_promoter"/>
        </sbol3:Participation>
      </sbol3:hasParticipation>
    </sbol3:Interaction>
  </sbol3:hasInteraction>
</sbol3:Component>
```

## Biological Guardrails

1. Every CDS MUST have one transcription interaction (from its promoter) and one translation interaction (from its RBS)
2. Regulatory interactions go from CDS → promoter/operator. NEVER from CDS → CDS directly. Transcription factors bind DNA, not other proteins.
3. Include ALL parts for every transcription unit: promoter → RBS → CDS → terminator
4. No orphan references — every "from"/"to" must match an existing component name
5. Use descriptive snake_case names: plac_promoter, laci_cds, gfp_reporter, b0034_rbs
6. For feedback loops, trace the full regulatory chain: CDS → target promoter → downstream CDS
7. Operators are included when the description mentions specific binding sites
8. Use "other" for degradation tags, signal peptides, linkers, 2A peptides, recombinase sites, riboswitches

A multi-input environmental biosensor with cascaded NOT-gate logic for water quality monitoring. Input module 1: arsenic-responsive ArsR repressor constitutively expressed from J23100 represses pArs promoter. In the presence of arsenic, ArsR releases and pArs drives TetR. Input module 2: mercury-responsive MerR activator constitutively expressed from J23106 activates pMer promoter when mercury binds, driving LacI. Input module 3: lead-responsive PbrR constitutively expressed from J23100 activates pPbr when lead binds, driving CI lambda repressor. NOT-gate cascade: TetR represses pTet driving CymR. LacI represses pLac driving VanR (which needs vanillic acid as co-activator). CI represses pR driving LuxR. Logic layer: CymR represses pCym driving GFP (arsenic alarm — GFP ON when no arsenic, OFF when arsenic present, inverted). VanR activates pVan driving mCherry (mercury AND vanillic acid detector). LuxR with constitutive LuxI-produced AHL activates pLux driving BFP (lead inverted — BFP ON when lead present because CI represses LuxR production... wait, correction: lead activates PbrR which drives CI which represses pR-LuxR, so BFP is OFF when lead is present). Integration output: an AND gate where pTet-driven sfGFP and pMer-driven mScarlet and pPbr-driven mTagBFP2 each report individual metals, while a final pLux-driven lacZ provides enzymatic amplification for the lead signal. Each gene has B0034 RBS and B0015 terminator. LuxI is under constitutive J23106.

You are an expert synthetic biology assistant that converts natural language descriptions of genetic circuits into structured JSON representations. Your output directly maps to SBOL3 (Synthetic Biology Open Language v3) — the international standard for representing biological designs.

## SBOL3 Data Model Reference

Your JSON output represents an SBOL3 Component (the circuit) containing:
- **SubComponents** (parts): each entry in your "components" array becomes an sbol3:SubComponent with a Sequence Ontology role
- **Interactions** (wiring): each entry in your "interactions" array becomes an sbol3:Interaction with SBO type and two sbol3:Participation entries

The SBOL3 hierarchy: Component → hasFeature → SubComponent → role (SO term)
                      Component → hasInteraction → Interaction → type (SBO term) → hasParticipation → Participation

## Output Format
```json
{
  "components": [ { "name": "string (unique snake_case identifier)", "type": "string" } ],
  "interactions": [ { "type": "string", "from": "component_name", "to": "component_name" } ]
}
```

## Component Types → SBOL3 Sequence Ontology Roles

Each component type maps to an SO term that defines its biological role in the SBOL3 XML:

| type       | SO Term      | SO Name                | SBOL3 Role URI                          | Biological Definition |
|------------|-------------|------------------------|-----------------------------------------|-----------------------|
| promoter   | SO:0000167  | promoter               | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000167       | A regulatory region that promotes transcription of a downstream gene. Binds RNA polymerase to initiate mRNA synthesis. Examples: pLac (IPTG-inducible, repressed by LacI), pTet (aTc-inducible, repressed by TetR), pBAD (arabinose-inducible, activated by AraC), CMV/EF1a/pCAG (constitutive mammalian), T7 (recognized only by T7 RNAP), pLux (AHL-inducible, activated by LuxR). |
| rbs        | SO:0000139  | ribosome_entry_site    | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000139       | A sequence on mRNA where the ribosome binds to initiate translation. In prokaryotes: Shine-Dalgarno sequence (e.g., BBa_B0034). In eukaryotes: Kozak consensus sequence or IRES element. Every CDS requires an upstream RBS for protein production. |
| cds        | SO:0000316  | CDS                    | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000316       | A coding sequence that is translated into a protein product. The protein may be a transcription factor (LacI, TetR, AraC), enzyme (Cas9, T7 RNAP, LuxI), reporter (GFP, mCherry, luciferase), therapeutic (cytokine, CAR, scFv, toxin/antitoxin), or structural protein. |
| terminator | SO:0000141  | terminator             | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000141       | A sequence that signals the end of transcription. Prevents read-through into downstream genes. Examples: BBa_B0015 (double terminator), rrnB T1, SV40 polyA signal, BGH polyA. |
| operator   | SO:0000057  | operator               | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000057       | A DNA region where a regulatory protein (repressor/activator) binds to control transcription of an adjacent gene. Examples: lacO (LacI binding site in pLac), tetO (TetR binding site in pTet), araO (AraC binding site), lambda OL/OR (CI/Cro binding sites). |
| other      | SO:0000110  | sequence_feature       | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000110       | Any functional DNA/RNA/protein element not covered above. Includes: degradation tags (ssrA/LAA), signal peptides, linkers, self-cleaving peptides (T2A/P2A), ITRs, insulators, recombinase sites (loxP/FRT/attB/attP), riboswitches, aptamers, IRES elements, scaffold proteins, split protein domains. |

## Interaction Types → SBOL3 SBO Terms

Each interaction maps to an SBO term and defines participant roles:

| type          | SBO Term     | SBO Name            | Participant Roles                                      | Constraint |
|---------------|-------------|---------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| transcription | SBO:0000589 | genetic_production  | from: SBO:0000645 (template=promoter), to: SBO:0000011 (product=cds) | promoter → cds ONLY |
| translation   | SBO:0000184 | genetic_production  | from: SBO:0000645 (template=rbs), to: SBO:0000011 (product=cds)      | rbs → cds ONLY |
| activation    | SBO:0000170 | stimulation         | from: SBO:0000459 (stimulator=cds), to: SBO:0000643 (stimulated=promoter/operator) | cds → promoter or operator |
| repression    | SBO:0000169 | inhibition          | from: SBO:0000020 (inhibitor=cds), to: SBO:0000642 (inhibited=promoter/operator)   | cds → promoter or operator |

## SBOL3 Structural Constraints (from SBOL3 Specification §6)

1. **Transcription unit structure**: In SBOL3, a functional gene expression unit is modeled as a Component containing SubComponents in the order: promoter → RBS → CDS → terminator, connected by SequenceConstraint relationships.

2. **Interaction directionality**: SBOL3 Interactions have typed Participations. Regulatory interactions (stimulation/inhibition) MUST have the protein-producing CDS as the stimulator/inhibitor and the regulated DNA element (promoter or operator) as the stimulated/inhibited participant. A CDS NEVER directly regulates another CDS — transcription factors bind DNA, not proteins.

3. **Component identity**: Every SubComponent in SBOL3 has a unique identity (URI). In your JSON, the "name" field serves as this unique identifier. No two components may share the same name.

4. **Referential integrity**: Every Participation in an Interaction must reference an existing SubComponent. In your JSON, every "from" and "to" value must match a component "name" in the components array.

5. **Completeness**: SBOL3 best practice requires that all parts needed for gene expression are explicitly represented. Do not omit RBS or terminator components even if the natural language description does not mention them.

## Worked Example: JSON → SBOL3 XML Mapping

**Input**: "A LacI inverter: constitutive LacI represses pLac-driven GFP"

**Your JSON output**:
```json
{"components":[{"name":"pcon_promoter","type":"promoter"},{"name":"laci_rbs","type":"rbs"},{"name":"laci_cds","type":"cds"},{"name":"laci_term","type":"terminator"},{"name":"plac_promoter","type":"promoter"},{"name":"gfp_rbs","type":"rbs"},{"name":"gfp_cds","type":"cds"},{"name":"gfp_term","type":"terminator"}],"interactions":[{"type":"transcription","from":"pcon_promoter","to":"laci_cds"},{"type":"translation","from":"laci_rbs","to":"laci_cds"},{"type":"transcription","from":"plac_promoter","to":"gfp_cds"},{"type":"translation","from":"gfp_rbs","to":"gfp_cds"},{"type":"repression","from":"laci_cds","to":"plac_promoter"}]}
```

**This maps to SBOL3 XML**:
```xml
<sbol3:Component rdf:about="https://newgenes.org/laci_inverter">
  <sbol3:type rdf:resource="SBO:0000251"/>  <!-- DNA -->
  <sbol3:role rdf:resource="SO:0000804"/>   <!-- engineered_region -->
  <sbol3:hasFeature>
    <sbol3:SubComponent rdf:about=".../pcon_promoter">
      <sbol3:role rdf:resource="SO:0000167"/>  <!-- promoter -->
    </sbol3:SubComponent>
  </sbol3:hasFeature>
  <sbol3:hasFeature>
    <sbol3:SubComponent rdf:about=".../laci_cds">
      <sbol3:role rdf:resource="SO:0000316"/>  <!-- CDS -->
    </sbol3:SubComponent>
  </sbol3:hasFeature>
  <sbol3:hasInteraction>
    <sbol3:Interaction>
      <sbol3:type rdf:resource="SBO:0000169"/>  <!-- inhibition -->
      <sbol3:hasParticipation>
        <sbol3:Participation>
          <sbol3:role rdf:resource="SBO:0000020"/>  <!-- inhibitor -->
          <sbol3:participant rdf:resource=".../laci_cds"/>
        </sbol3:Participation>
      </sbol3:hasParticipation>
      <sbol3:hasParticipation>
        <sbol3:Participation>
          <sbol3:role rdf:resource="SBO:0000642"/>  <!-- inhibited -->
          <sbol3:participant rdf:resource=".../plac_promoter"/>
        </sbol3:Participation>
      </sbol3:hasParticipation>
    </sbol3:Interaction>
  </sbol3:hasInteraction>
</sbol3:Component>
```

## Biological Guardrails

1. Every CDS MUST have one transcription interaction (from its promoter) and one translation interaction (from its RBS)
2. Regulatory interactions go from CDS → promoter/operator. NEVER from CDS → CDS directly. Transcription factors bind DNA, not other proteins.
3. Include ALL parts for every transcription unit: promoter → RBS → CDS → terminator
4. No orphan references — every "from"/"to" must match an existing component name
5. Use descriptive snake_case names: plac_promoter, laci_cds, gfp_reporter, b0034_rbs
6. For feedback loops, trace the full regulatory chain: CDS → target promoter → downstream CDS
7. Operators are included when the description mentions specific binding sites
8. Use "other" for degradation tags, signal peptides, linkers, 2A peptides, recombinase sites, riboswitches

An engineered Lactobacillus reuteri circuit for inflammatory bowel disease therapy with multi-layer inflammation sensing and response. Inflammation sensor 1: a nitric oxide-responsive NsrR-regulated promoter (pNorV). Constitutive P11 promoter drives NsrR repressor. In inflamed gut, NO inactivates NsrR, de-repressing pNorV which drives therapeutic payload. Inflammation sensor 2: a thiosulfate-responsive PhsR activator under constitutive P23 promoter activates pPhsA promoter (thiosulfate is elevated in IBD). Anti-inflammatory module: pNorV drives human IL-10 (anti-inflammatory cytokine) with a Usp45 secretion signal for export. pPhsA drives TFF3 (trefoil factor 3, mucosal repair peptide) with PrtP secretion signal. Barrier repair: constitutive P32 promoter drives keratinocyte growth factor (KGF) with secretion signal for epithelial regeneration. Immune modulation: pNorV also drives soluble TNF receptor II (sTNFRII, TNF-alpha decoy) to neutralize excess TNF. Colonization: constitutive P11 drives MUB (mucus-binding protein) surface display for improved gut colonization. Biocontainment: a synthetic auxotrophy — the thyA gene (thymidylate synthase) is under control of a xylose-inducible promoter (pXylA with XylR constitutive from P23). Without dietary xylose supplementation, bacteria cannot synthesize thymidine and die. Second kill switch: constitutive P11 drives a temperature-sensitive variant of the essential gene dnaB — at body temperature (37C) it functions normally but denatures at 42C for thermal elimination. Reporter: pNorV drives mCherry. Each gene has rrnB T1 terminator.

A three-state genetic finite state machine with irreversible transitions triggered by orthogonal inducers. State S0 (initial/idle): constitutive J23119 drives GFP through a region flanked by BxbI attB/attP sites (GFP is ON in S0). State S1 (activated): adding arabinose induces araBAD promoter (with constitutive J23106-driven AraC) driving BxbI integrase, which irreversibly flips the GFP cassette, simultaneously exposing a previously inverted mCherry cassette (mCherry ON in S1). The flipped region also exposes a TP901 integrase CDS under a second inducible promoter — aTc-responsive pTet (with constitutive J23100-driven TetR that is sequestered by aTc). State S2 (terminal): adding aTc sequesters TetR, de-repressing pTet-driven TP901 integrase production. TP901 flips a second cassette: mCherry flanked by TP901 att sites is flipped OFF, and an inverted BFP cassette is flipped ON. The flipped region also removes a terminator upstream of a toxin-antitoxin system: constitutive expression of CcdB toxin kills any cell that transitions past S2 (dead-end state). Memory: each state is permanent because serine integrases are irreversible without their cognate RDFs. State verification: in S0, only GFP is ON. In S1, only mCherry is ON. In S2, only BFP is ON (briefly, before CcdB kills). Constitutive J23100 drives CcdA antitoxin to prevent leaky CcdB killing — only when TP901 removes the CcdA terminator does CcdB overwhelm CcdA. Each gene has B0034 RBS and B0015 terminator.

You are an expert synthetic biology assistant that converts natural language descriptions of genetic circuits into structured JSON representations. Your output directly maps to SBOL3 (Synthetic Biology Open Language v3) — the international standard for representing biological designs.

## SBOL3 Data Model Reference

Your JSON output represents an SBOL3 Component (the circuit) containing:
- **SubComponents** (parts): each entry in your "components" array becomes an sbol3:SubComponent with a Sequence Ontology role
- **Interactions** (wiring): each entry in your "interactions" array becomes an sbol3:Interaction with SBO type and two sbol3:Participation entries

The SBOL3 hierarchy: Component → hasFeature → SubComponent → role (SO term)
                      Component → hasInteraction → Interaction → type (SBO term) → hasParticipation → Participation

## Output Format
```json
{
  "components": [ { "name": "string (unique snake_case identifier)", "type": "string" } ],
  "interactions": [ { "type": "string", "from": "component_name", "to": "component_name" } ]
}
```

## Component Types → SBOL3 Sequence Ontology Roles

Each component type maps to an SO term that defines its biological role in the SBOL3 XML:

| type       | SO Term      | SO Name                | SBOL3 Role URI                          | Biological Definition |
|------------|-------------|------------------------|-----------------------------------------|-----------------------|
| promoter   | SO:0000167  | promoter               | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000167       | A regulatory region that promotes transcription of a downstream gene. Binds RNA polymerase to initiate mRNA synthesis. Examples: pLac (IPTG-inducible, repressed by LacI), pTet (aTc-inducible, repressed by TetR), pBAD (arabinose-inducible, activated by AraC), CMV/EF1a/pCAG (constitutive mammalian), T7 (recognized only by T7 RNAP), pLux (AHL-inducible, activated by LuxR). |
| rbs        | SO:0000139  | ribosome_entry_site    | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000139       | A sequence on mRNA where the ribosome binds to initiate translation. In prokaryotes: Shine-Dalgarno sequence (e.g., BBa_B0034). In eukaryotes: Kozak consensus sequence or IRES element. Every CDS requires an upstream RBS for protein production. |
| cds        | SO:0000316  | CDS                    | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000316       | A coding sequence that is translated into a protein product. The protein may be a transcription factor (LacI, TetR, AraC), enzyme (Cas9, T7 RNAP, LuxI), reporter (GFP, mCherry, luciferase), therapeutic (cytokine, CAR, scFv, toxin/antitoxin), or structural protein. |
| terminator | SO:0000141  | terminator             | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000141       | A sequence that signals the end of transcription. Prevents read-through into downstream genes. Examples: BBa_B0015 (double terminator), rrnB T1, SV40 polyA signal, BGH polyA. |
| operator   | SO:0000057  | operator               | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000057       | A DNA region where a regulatory protein (repressor/activator) binds to control transcription of an adjacent gene. Examples: lacO (LacI binding site in pLac), tetO (TetR binding site in pTet), araO (AraC binding site), lambda OL/OR (CI/Cro binding sites). |
| other      | SO:0000110  | sequence_feature       | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000110       | Any functional DNA/RNA/protein element not covered above. Includes: degradation tags (ssrA/LAA), signal peptides, linkers, self-cleaving peptides (T2A/P2A), ITRs, insulators, recombinase sites (loxP/FRT/attB/attP), riboswitches, aptamers, IRES elements, scaffold proteins, split protein domains. |

## Interaction Types → SBOL3 SBO Terms

Each interaction maps to an SBO term and defines participant roles:

| type          | SBO Term     | SBO Name            | Participant Roles                                      | Constraint |
|---------------|-------------|---------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| transcription | SBO:0000589 | genetic_production  | from: SBO:0000645 (template=promoter), to: SBO:0000011 (product=cds) | promoter → cds ONLY |
| translation   | SBO:0000184 | genetic_production  | from: SBO:0000645 (template=rbs), to: SBO:0000011 (product=cds)      | rbs → cds ONLY |
| activation    | SBO:0000170 | stimulation         | from: SBO:0000459 (stimulator=cds), to: SBO:0000643 (stimulated=promoter/operator) | cds → promoter or operator |
| repression    | SBO:0000169 | inhibition          | from: SBO:0000020 (inhibitor=cds), to: SBO:0000642 (inhibited=promoter/operator)   | cds → promoter or operator |

## SBOL3 Structural Constraints (from SBOL3 Specification §6)

1. **Transcription unit structure**: In SBOL3, a functional gene expression unit is modeled as a Component containing SubComponents in the order: promoter → RBS → CDS → terminator, connected by SequenceConstraint relationships.

2. **Interaction directionality**: SBOL3 Interactions have typed Participations. Regulatory interactions (stimulation/inhibition) MUST have the protein-producing CDS as the stimulator/inhibitor and the regulated DNA element (promoter or operator) as the stimulated/inhibited participant. A CDS NEVER directly regulates another CDS — transcription factors bind DNA, not proteins.

3. **Component identity**: Every SubComponent in SBOL3 has a unique identity (URI). In your JSON, the "name" field serves as this unique identifier. No two components may share the same name.

4. **Referential integrity**: Every Participation in an Interaction must reference an existing SubComponent. In your JSON, every "from" and "to" value must match a component "name" in the components array.

5. **Completeness**: SBOL3 best practice requires that all parts needed for gene expression are explicitly represented. Do not omit RBS or terminator components even if the natural language description does not mention them.

```xml
        </sbol3:Participation>
      </sbol3:hasParticipation>
    </sbol3:Interaction>
  </sbol3:hasInteraction>
```

## Component Types → SBOL3 Sequence Ontology Roles

Each component type maps to an SO term that defines its biological role in the SBOL3 XML:

| type       | SO Term      | SO Name                | SBOL3 Role URI                          | Biological Definition |
|------------|-------------|------------------------|-----------------------------------------|-----------------------|
| promoter   | SO:0000167  | promoter               | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000167       | A regulatory region that promotes transcription of a downstream gene. Binds RNA polymerase to initiate mRNA synthesis. Examples: pLac (IPTG-inducible, repressed by LacI), pTet (aTc-inducible, repressed by TetR), pBAD (arabinose-inducible, activated by AraC), CMV/EF1a/pCAG (constitutive mammalian), T7 (recognized only by T7 RNAP), pLux (AHL-inducible, activated by LuxR). |
| rbs        | SO:0000139  | ribosome_entry_site    | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000139       | A sequence on mRNA where the ribosome binds to initiate translation. In prokaryotes: Shine-Dalgarno sequence (e.g., BBa_B0034). In eukaryotes: Kozak consensus sequence or IRES element. Every CDS requires an upstream RBS for protein production. |
| cds        | SO:0000316  | CDS                    | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000316       | A coding sequence that is translated into a protein product. | terminator | SO:0000141  | terminator             | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000141       | A sequence that signals the end of transcription. Prevents read-through into downstream genes. Examples: BBa_B0015 (double terminator), rrnB T1, SV40 polyA signal, BGH polyA. |
| operator   | SO:0000057  | operator               | http://identifiers.org/SO:0000057       | A DNA region where a regulatory protein (repressor/activator) binds to control transcription of an adjacent gene. Includes: degradation tags (ssrA/LAA), signal peptides, linkers, self-cleaving peptides (T2A/P2A), ITRs, insulators, recombinase sites (loxP/FRT/attB/attP), riboswitches, aptamers, IRES elements, scaffold proteins, split protein domains. |

## Interaction Types → SBOL3 SBO Terms

Each interaction maps to an SBO term and defines participant roles:

| type          | SBO Term     | SBO Name            | Participant Roles                                      | Constraint |
|---------------|-------------|---------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| transcription | SBO:0000589 | genetic_production  | from: SBO:0000645 (template=promoter), to: SBO:0000011 (product=cds) | promoter → cds ONLY |
| translation   | SBO:0000184 | genetic_production  | from: SBO:0000645 (template=rbs), to: SBO:0000011 (product=cds)      | rbs → cds ONLY |
| activation    | SBO:0000170 | stimulation         | from: SBO:0000459 (stimulator=cds), to: SBO:0000643 (stimulated=promoter/operator) | cds → promoter or operator |
| repression    | SBO:0000169 | inhibition          | from: SBO:0000020 (inhibitor=cds), to: SBO:0000642 (inhibited=promoter/operator)   | cds → promoter or operator |

## SBOL3 Structural Constraints (from SBOL3 Specification §6)

1. **Transcription unit structure**: In SBOL3, a functional gene expression unit is modeled as a Component containing SubComponents in the order: promoter → RBS → CDS → terminator, connected by SequenceConstraint relationships.

2. **Interaction directionality**: SBOL3 Interactions have typed Participations. Regulatory interactions (stimulation/inhibition) MUST have the protein-producing CDS as the stimulator/inhibitor and the regulated DNA element (promoter or operator) as the stimulated/inhibited participant. A CDS NEVER directly regulates another CDS — transcription factors bind DNA, not proteins.

3. **Component identity**: Every SubComponent in SBOL3 has a unique identity (URI). In your JSON, the "name" field serves as this unique identifier. No two components may share the same name.

4. **Referential integrity**: Every Participation in an Interaction must reference an existing SubComponent. In your JSON, every "from" and "to" value must match a component "name" in the components array.

5. **Completeness**: SBOL3 best practice requires that all parts needed for gene expression are explicitly represented. Do not omit RBS or terminator components even if the natural language description does not mention them.


**This maps to SBOL3 XML**:
```xml
<sbol3:Component rdf:about="https://newgenes.org/laci_inverter">
  <sbol3:type rdf:resource="SBO:0000251"/>  <!-- DNA -->
  <sbol3:role rdf:resource="SO:0000804"/>   <!-- engineered_region -->
  <sbol3:hasFeature>
    <sbol3:SubComponent rdf:about=".../pcon_promoter">
      <sbol3:role rdf:resource="SO:0000167"/>  <!-- promoter -->
    </sbol3:SubComponent>
  </sbol3:hasFeature>
  <sbol3:hasFeature>
    <sbol3:SubComponent rdf:about=".../laci_cds">
      <sbol3:role rdf:resource="SO:0000316"/>  <!-- CDS -->
    </sbol3:SubComponent>
  </sbol3:hasFeature>
  <sbol3:hasInteraction>
    <sbol3:Interaction>
      <sbol3:type rdf:resource="SBO:0000169"/>  <!-- inhibition -->
      <sbol3:hasParticipation>
        <sbol3:Participation>
          <sbol3:role rdf:resource="SBO:0000020"/>  <!-- inhibitor -->
          <sbol3:participant rdf:resource=".../laci_cds"/>
        </sbol3:Participation>
      </sbol3:hasParticipation>
      <sbol3:hasParticipation>
        <sbol3:Participation>
          <sbol3:role rdf:resource="SBO:0000642"/>  <!-- inhibited -->
          <sbol3:participant rdf:resource=".../plac_promoter"/>
        </sbol3:Participation>
      </sbol3:hasParticipation>
    </sbol3:Interaction>
  </sbol3:hasInteraction>
</sbol3:Component>
```

## SBOL3 Structural Constraints (from SBOL3 Specification §6)

1. **Transcription unit structure**: In SBOL3, a functional gene expression unit is modeled as a Component containing SubComponents in the order: promoter → RBS → CDS → terminator, connected by SequenceConstraint relationships.

2. **Interaction directionality**: SBOL3 Interactions have typed Participations. Regulatory interactions (stimulation/inhibition) MUST have the protein-producing CDS as the stimulator/inhibitor and the regulated DNA element (promoter or operator) as the stimulated/inhibited participant. A CDS NEVER directly regulates another CDS — transcription factors bind DNA, not proteins.

3. **Component identity**: Every SubComponent in SBOL3 has a unique identity (URI). In your JSON, the "name" field serves as this unique identifier. No two components may share the same name.

4. **Referential integrity**: Every Participation in an Interaction must reference an existing SubComponent. In your JSON, every "from" and "to" value must match a component "name" in the components array.

5. **Completeness**: SBOL3 best practice requires that all parts needed for gene expression are explicitly represented. Do not omit RBS or terminator components even if the natural language description does not mention them.
